Name: Notifiable Diseases & Conditions
Description: Individual reportable communicable diseases and conditions, one term per Alberta disease-management guideline, grouped by causative agent.

Name: Disease Syndrome Groups
Description: Umbrella groupings that bundle several individual diseases under a clinically or epidemiologically meaningful heading.

Name: Surveillance & Reporting Concepts
Description: Vocabulary of notification, reporting, case classification, and the roles and forms used in public-health surveillance.

Name: Transmission & Epidemiology Concepts
Description: Modes of transmission and core epidemiological measures used across the disease guidelines.

Name: Public Health Management Concepts
Description: Interventions and precautions applied to manage cases, contacts, and outbreaks.

Name: Clinical & Laboratory Concepts
Description: Clinical and diagnostic vocabulary, including laboratory test methods.

Name: Regulatory & Governance
Description: Legislation, regulation, and policy instruments governing communicable disease management in Alberta.

Name: Bacterial Diseases
Description: Diseases caused by bacterial agents.

Name: Viral Diseases
Description: Diseases caused by viral agents.

Name: Parasitic Diseases
Description: Diseases caused by protozoan or helminth parasites.

Name: Fungal Diseases
Description: Diseases caused by fungal agents.

Name: Prion Diseases
Description: Transmissible spongiform encephalopathies caused by prions.

Name: Toxin-Mediated Illness
Description: Illness caused by pre-formed microbial toxins or biotoxins rather than active infection.

Name: Syndromes & Conditions
Description: Reportable clinical syndromes and surveillance conditions where the causative agent varies, is multiple, or is under investigation.

Name: Arboviral Infections
Description: Arthropod-borne (mosquito and tick) viral infections; includes Chikungunya, Dengue, Eastern/Western/St. Louis/Japanese/Powassan encephalitis, Jamestown Canyon and Snowshoe Hare viruses, West Nile Virus, Yellow Fever, and Zika.

Name: Viral Haemorrhagic Fevers
Description: Severe viral illnesses with bleeding manifestations; includes Argentine, Crimean-Congo, Ebola, Lassa, Marburg, and Rift Valley haemorrhagic fevers.

Name: Viral Hepatitis
Description: Inflammatory liver disease caused by hepatitis viruses; includes Hepatitis A, B, C, and E.

Name: Coronaviruses
Description: Reportable coronavirus infections; includes COVID-19, MERS/SARS, and Novel coronaviruses.

Name: Influenza
Description: Reportable influenza infections; includes Seasonal, Avian, and Novel influenza.

Name: Encephalitides
Description: Inflammatory infections of the brain, predominantly arboviral (Eastern/Western/St. Louis/Japanese/Powassan encephalitis).

Name: Invasive Bacterial Disease
Description: Bacterial infections of normally sterile sites; includes invasive Meningococcal, Pneumococcal, Haemophilus influenzae, and Group A Streptococcal disease.

Name: Sexually Transmitted & Bloodborne Infections
Description: Infections transmitted sexually or through blood; includes Chlamydia, Gonorrhea, Syphilis, HIV, Chancroid, Lymphogranuloma Venereum, Non-Gonococcal Urethritis, and Hepatitis B and C.

Name: Notifiable Disease
Description: A disease that, by law, must be reported to public-health authorities when identified.

Name: Reportable Disease
Description: A communicable disease that must be reported by clinicians or laboratories under Alberta's Public Health Act and regulations.

Name: Notifiable Disease Report (NDR)
Description: The standard form and process used to report notifiable diseases to the Medical Officer of Health.

Name: Alberta Outbreak Reporting Form (AORF)
Description: The form used to report outbreaks and outbreak-associated cases.

Name: Case Definition
Description: The standardised set of clinical and laboratory criteria used to classify a case for surveillance.

Name: Confirmed Case
Description: A case meeting the laboratory (and where required clinical) criteria for confirmation in the case definition.

Name: Probable Case
Description: A case meeting clinical and epidemiological criteria but lacking definitive laboratory confirmation.

Name: Suspect Case
Description: A case with a clinical presentation compatible with the disease but not yet meeting probable or confirmed criteria.

Name: Outbreak
Description: An occurrence of cases in excess of normal expectancy; reportable to the Chief Medical Officer of Health.

Name: Fastest Means Possible (FMP)
Description: A reporting urgency requiring immediate notification by the quickest available method.

Name: Medical Officer of Health (MOH)
Description: The designated public-health physician responsible for disease control in a health region.

Name: Chief Medical Officer of Health (CMOH)
Description: The provincial public-health authority to whom outbreaks and certain diseases are reported.

Name: STI Director
Description: The provincial authority to whom sexually transmitted infections are reported under the laboratory reporting reference.

Name: Airborne Transmission
Description: Spread by dissemination of droplet nuclei (under 5 microns) that remain suspended in air and may be inhaled by people.

Name: Droplet Transmission
Description: Spread by respiratory droplets (5 microns or larger) expelled over short distances during coughing, sneezing, or talking.

Name: Contact Transmission
Description: Spread through direct (body-to-body) or indirect (via contaminated objects) contact with an infected or colonised individual.

Name: Vector-Borne Transmission
Description: Spread by an arthropod vector such as a mosquito or tick.

Name: Foodborne / Waterborne (Enteric) Transmission
Description: Spread through ingestion of contaminated food or water; characteristic of enteric pathogens.

Name: Bloodborne Transmission
Description: Spread through exposure to infected blood or blood products.

Name: Sexual Transmission
Description: Spread through sexual contact.

Name: Congenital / Perinatal Transmission
Description: Spread from an infected mother to her fetus or newborn before, during, or shortly after birth.

Name: Zoonotic Transmission
Description: Spread from animals to humans.

Name: Incubation Period
Description: The interval between exposure to an infectious agent and the onset of symptoms.

Name: Period of Communicability
Description: The interval during which an infected person can transmit the agent to others.

Name: Reservoir
Description: The habitat (human, animal, or environmental) in which an infectious agent normally lives and multiplies.

Name: Vector
Description: A living organism that transmits an infectious agent from one host to another.

Name: Endemic / Epidemic
Description: Endemic: continuously prevalent in a population or area. Epidemic: occurrence clearly in excess of normal expectancy.

Name: Airborne Precautions
Description: Controls for airborne pathogens, including N95 respirators, hand hygiene, and negative-pressure isolation.

Name: Droplet Precautions
Description: Controls for droplet-spread pathogens, including surgical masks and eye protection within close range of the patient.

Name: Contact Precautions
Description: Controls to reduce direct and indirect contact spread, including gloves, gowns, hand hygiene, and single-room accommodation.

Name: Isolation
Description: Separation of an infectious person from others to prevent transmission.

Name: Quarantine
Description: Restriction of movement of exposed but asymptomatic individuals during the incubation period.

Name: Contact Tracing
Description: Identification and follow-up of persons who may have been exposed to a case.

Name: Prophylaxis
Description: Preventive treatment given to exposed or at-risk individuals, including pre-exposure and post-exposure prophylaxis.

Name: Exclusion
Description: Temporary removal of an infectious person from a setting such as school, daycare, or work.

Name: Case Management
Description: The public-health follow-up and care coordination undertaken for a confirmed or probable case.

Name: Outbreak Management
Description: The coordinated public-health response to control an outbreak.

Name: Etiology
Description: The causative agent or origin of a disease.

Name: Clinical Presentation
Description: The signs and symptoms with which a disease typically manifests.

Name: Diagnosis
Description: The process and criteria for identifying a disease in an individual.

Name: Laboratory Confirmation
Description: Detection of an agent or its markers by an approved laboratory method, satisfying the case definition.

Name: Treatment
Description: The clinical therapy indicated for a disease.

Name: Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR)
Description: A molecular method that detects pathogen nucleic acid.

Name: Serology
Description: Detection of antibodies or antigens in serum to establish infection or immune status.

Name: Culture
Description: Growth and isolation of an organism from a clinical specimen.

Name: Direct Fluorescent Antibody (DFA)
Description: A test using fluorescent-tagged antibodies to detect microbial antigens in body fluids.

Name: Respiratory Pathogen Panel (RPP)
Description: A multiplex laboratory panel that simultaneously tests for multiple respiratory pathogens.

Name: Public Health Act (Alberta)
Description: Alberta's governing legislation for public health, including communicable disease control (RSA 2000, Chapter P-37).

Name: Notifiable Disease Regulation
Description: The regulation specifying which diseases are notifiable and the associated reporting obligations.

Name: Notifiable Disease Guidelines
Description: The Alberta Public Health disease-management guideline series providing standardised case definitions and management for each notifiable disease.

Name: Open Government Licence – Alberta
Description: The licence under which the disease-management guidelines are published.
